context.description: English source strings for the common namespace.

messages.destiny.use.error.amount-range: You can only use between {{minimum}} and {{maximum}} points at a time.
messages.destiny.use.error.not-enough-points_one: There aren't enough {{pointType}} points to use. You asked for {{requested}}, but there's only {{available}}.
messages.destiny.use.error.not-enough-points_othe: There aren't enough {{pointType}} points to use. You asked for {{requested}}, but there are only {{available}}.

messages.game-language.description: Sets the language for newly generated shared game output. Existing game history is not changed.
messages.game-language.label: Game Language
messages.language.description: Language for app labels. Your characters keep their own skill names.
messages.language.label: Language

context.description: English source strings for the settings namespace.
Account-localized label for the dark-side destiny-point pool inside a private Discord validation reply.
context.state: The invoking account selected the dark-side pool but the private command validation failed.
translatorNotes: This label uses the invoking account or Discord locale.
Use the established Star Wars rules term in the target language.

context.description: Account-localized label for the game-master-controlled story-point pool inside a private Discord validation reply.
context.state: The invoking account selected the GM pool but the private command validation failed.
translatorNotes: This label uses the invoking account or Discord locale.
Use the conventional local abbreviation for game master.

context.description: Account-localized label for the light-side destiny-point pool inside a private Discord validation reply.
context.state: The invoking account selected the light-side pool but the private command validation failed.
translatorNotes: This label uses the invoking account or Discord locale.
Use the established Star Wars rules term in the target language.

context.description: Account-localized label for the player-controlled story-point pool inside a private Discord validation reply.
context.state: The invoking account selected the player pool but the private command validation failed.
translatorNotes: This label uses the invoking account or Discord locale.
Use a noun phrase that reads naturally inside the private insufficient-points sentence.

context.description: Game-localized label for the dark-side destiny-point pool inside a shared Discord confirmation.
context.state: The dark-side pool was changed successfully and the channel receives the shared confirmation.
translatorNotes: This label uses the game's language, not the invoking account's language.
Use the established Star Wars rules term in the target language.

context.description: Game-localized label for the game-master-controlled story-point pool inside a shared Discord confirmation.
context.state: The GM pool was changed successfully and the channel receives the shared confirmation.
translatorNotes: This label uses the game's language, not the invoking account's language.
Use the conventional local abbreviation for game master.

context.description: Game-localized label for the light-side destiny-point pool inside a shared Discord confirmation.
context.state: The light-side pool was changed successfully and the channel receives the shared confirmation.
translatorNotes: This label uses the game's language, not the invoking account's language.
Use the established Star Wars rules term in the target language.

context.description: Game-localized label for the player-controlled story-point pool inside a shared Discord confirmation.
context.state: The player pool was changed successfully and the channel receives the shared confirmation.
translatorNotes: This label uses the game's language, not the invoking account's language.
Use a noun phrase that reads naturally after the shared confirmation count.

strings.source: You can only use between {{minimum}} and {{maximum}} points at a time.

context.description: Private Discord validation reply shown when an account requests an unsupported number of story or destiny points.

maximum.meaning: Largest number of points allowed in one use command.

minimum.meaning: Smallest number of points allowed in one use command.

context.route: Discord slash commands /story use and /destiny use
context.state: The invoking account supplied an amount below the minimum or above the maximum, before any game state changed.
translatorNotes: This reply is ephemeral and only the invoking account can see it.
Keep both numeric placeholders and make the inclusive range clear.

strings.source: There aren't enough {{pointType}} points to use. You asked for {{requested}}, but there's only {{available}}.

context.description: Private Discord error explaining that the selected game pool contains fewer points than the account requested, when exactly one point remains.

available.meaning: Number of matching points currently available in the shared game pool.

pointType.meaning: Prelocalized app-owned point-pool label rendered with the same account translator as this private reply.

requested.meaning: Number of points the invoking account asked to use.

context.route: Discord slash commands /story use and /destiny use
context.state: The selected shared pool has exactly one point, fewer than the requested amount, and no points were changed.
translatorNotes: This reply is ephemeral and uses the invoking account's language, not the game's language.
The pointType value is already localized by a discord:destiny.point-type key and is not user-authored text.
Keep the distinction between requested and available counts.

strings.source: There aren't enough {{pointType}} points to use. You asked for {{requested}}, but there are only {{available}}.

context.description: Private Discord error explaining that the selected game pool contains fewer points than the account requested, when zero or multiple points remain.

available.meaning: Number of matching points currently available in the shared game pool.

pointType.meaning: Prelocalized app-owned point-pool label rendered with the same account translator as this private reply.

requested.meaning: Number of points the invoking account asked to use.

context.route: Discord slash commands /story use and /destiny use
context.state: The selected shared pool has zero or multiple points, fewer than the requested amount, and no points were changed.
translatorNotes: This reply is ephemeral and uses the invoking account's language, not the game's language.
The pointType value is already localized by a discord:destiny.point-type key and is not user-authored text.
Keep the distinction between requested and available counts.

context.description: Discord embed heading confirming that one story or destiny point was used in the shared game.

count.meaning: Number of points used by the command.

pointType.meaning: Prelocalized app-owned point-pool label rendered with the same game translator as this shared Discord heading.

context.route: Discord slash commands /story use and /destiny use
context.state: Exactly one point changed pools and the bot is showing the updated shared game state.
translatorNotes: This heading is visible to the Discord channel and therefore uses the game's language.
The pointType value is already localized by a discord:destiny.point-type key and is not user-authored text.
Keep the confirmation concise because Discord embed author names allow at most 256 characters.

context.description: Discord embed heading confirming that multiple story or destiny points were used in the shared game.

count.meaning: Number of points used by the command.

pointType.meaning: Prelocalized app-owned point-pool label rendered with the same game translator as this shared Discord heading.

context.route: Discord slash commands /story use and /destiny use
context.state: Two or more points changed pools and the bot is showing the updated shared game state.
translatorNotes: This heading is visible to the Discord channel and therefore uses the game's language.
The pointType value is already localized by a discord:destiny.point-type key and is not user-authored text.
Keep the confirmation concise because Discord embed author names allow at most 256 characters.

context.description: Label for the proactive initiative skill function in NDS character rules.
context.state: The initiative skill function selector is visible.
translatorNotes: Proactive describes initiative taken while the character is prepared and acting deliberately.

context.description: Label for the reactive initiative skill function in NDS character rules.
context.state: The initiative skill function selector is visible.
translatorNotes: Reactive describes initiative taken in response to an unexpected event.

context.description: Name of English in account and game language pickers.
context.state: An account or game language picker is open.
translatorNotes: Use the language name as normally written in the current interface language.

context.description: Name of Spanish in account and game language pickers.
context.state: An account or game language picker is open. Spanish is currently a preview language.
translatorNotes: Use the language name as normally written in the current interface language.

context.description: Name of French in account and game language pickers.
context.state: An account or game language picker is open. French is currently a preview language.
translatorNotes: Use the language name as normally written in the current interface language.

context.description: Section label for choosing whether an NDS character skill is used for proactive or reactive initiative.
context.state: The skill modal is open for a skill that can be assigned an initiative function.
translatorNotes: Initiative is the encounter turn-order mechanic.
This label introduces the Proactive and Reactive choices for one character skill; it is not a tracker heading.

context.description: Recipient-personal browser push framing that summarizes who flipped a story point in the game.

authorName.meaning: Display name of the person who flipped the story point.

context.route: Browser push payloads built by the web app and import server
context.state: A game member flips a story point and each recipient receives notification framing in their account locale.
translatorNotes: Translate only the personal notification framing. The stored shared event text remains in the game's language.
Keep the author name placeholder unchanged.

context.description: Shared game output announcing that one story point moved between the configured pools.

count.meaning: Number of story points moved between pools.

fromLabel.meaning: Game theme label for the pool the point moved from.

pointLabel.meaning: Game theme label for a story point.

toLabel.meaning: Game theme label for the pool the point moved to.

context.state: Exactly one story point is flipped between the game's configured pools.
translatorNotes: Keep all placeholders unchanged.
The three label placeholders are configured by the game and must not be translated.

context.description: Shared game output announcing that multiple story points moved between the configured pools.

count.meaning: Number of story points moved between pools.

fromLabel.meaning: Game theme label for the pool the points moved from.

pointLabel.meaning: Game theme label for a story point.

toLabel.meaning: Game theme label for the pool the points moved to.

context.state: Two or more story points are flipped between the game's configured pools.
translatorNotes: Keep all placeholders unchanged.
The three label placeholders are configured by the game and must not be translated.

strings.source: Sets the language for newly generated shared game output. Existing game history is not changed.

context.description: Explains that the selected game language affects only shared game output generated after the setting is saved.
context.state: Game settings are open and the game language picker is visible.
translatorNotes: Newly generated shared game output includes persisted system-authored chat messages and notifications created for the game after this setting is saved.
Existing game history keeps the rendered text that was stored when each entry was created.
Do not imply that user-authored character, item, or chat text will be translated.

context.description: Label for the GM-controlled language picker in game settings.
context.state: Game settings are open. The control is editable by a GM and read-only for other game members.
translatorNotes: This is one language shared by the game, not the signed-in user's personal interface language.
Keep Game distinct from Account Language.

context.description: Placeholder shown by the game language picker when it has no visible selection.
context.state: Game settings are open and the game language control has no visible selection.
translatorNotes: Use an instruction that asks the GM to choose one language for the game.

strings.source: Language for app labels. Your characters keep their own skill names.

context.description: Supporting copy below the account language picker. Clarifies that user-authored skill names are not renamed.
context.state: The Language section is visible.

screenshot.caption: Account language field with the French preview selected and English fallback copy visible.

translatorNotes: Skill names means names entered or customized by players, not built-in app labels.

context.description: Label for the account language picker.
context.state: The Language section is visible.

screenshot.caption: Account language field with the French preview selected and English fallback copy visible.

translatorNotes: Use the standard term for the language selected for the application interface.

context.description: Formats a preview-stage language name in account and game language pickers so users know that its catalog is incomplete.

languageName.meaning: The localized display name of the language offered by this option.

context.state: An account or game language picker is open and the option is available for preview before its catalog is complete.

screenshot.caption: Account language field with the French preview selected and English fallback copy visible.

translatorNotes: Keep the preview status visibly attached to the language name.
The placeholder is another translated interface label, not user-authored text.
Reorder the placeholder and preview status if that reads more naturally in the target language.
